title: Repair live backend working tree drift and rerun phase4 formal workflow baseline validation
objective: Fix the live backend drift on the deployed server so the release commit's formal activation path is actually what the API service is running, then rerun phase4 post-deploy validation. Do not continue to phase5 Codex connectivity until phase4 passes.

mandatory_references.why: Use the latest structured result as the authoritative blocker statement and current stop point.
This is the direct evidence that live file contents do not match the release commit even though git HEAD does.
Use the known-good deployment context: server repo root, compose path, API service name, and release commit.
One of the three files proven to be drifting on the live server.
One of the three files proven to be drifting on the live server.
One of the three files proven to be drifting on the live server.

required_work.description: Reconfirm the exact drift scope on the live server. Verify that git HEAD is still the release commit and that the live contents of community.py, group_session.py, and protocol_manager.py still do not match the release commit.

Determine why live file contents diverged from the release commit. Check for bind-mounted source, local hot edits, uncommitted remote edits, copied overlay files, or restart path mistakes. Stop at the first real source of drift.
Exact-sync the live server copies of community.py, group_session.py, and protocol_manager.py to the release commit contents only. Do not broaden to unrelated files.
Restart only the API service needed to pick up the corrected files. Do not redeploy the whole stack if an API-only restart is sufficient.
Rerun phase4 post-deploy validation from the current group state. Verify V1_api_health, V2_manager_only_bootstrap, and especially V3_formal_workflow_baseline.
If V3 now passes, stop and report phase4 completed. If V3 still fails, stop at the first real blocker and do not continue to phase5 Codex connectivity.

do_not: Do not continue to phase5 Codex connectivity until phase4 passes.
Do not treat this as a generic executor outage unless fresh evidence proves that.
Do not redeploy the full stack if an API-only sync and restart is enough.
working_principles: This is a release-blocking drift repair, not a feature task.
Use exact file hashes and exact file contents as evidence, not git HEAD alone.
Repair the smallest live surface necessary to restore release-commit behavior.
Stop at the first real blocker.
acceptance: Live community.py, group_session.py, and protocol_manager.py match the release commit contents.
API service is healthy after restart.
Task stops before phase5 unless phase4 is fully green.
